title: Judges: Cycle of Rebellion
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of judges: cycle of rebellion and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study judges: cycle of rebellion and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: What recurring four-stage cycle appears throughout the book of Judges? Answer: Israel sins by serving other gods, God sends an oppressor, Israel cries out, God raises a judge to deliver them, there is peace, and then Israel sins again.
segments.correct_answer: sin, oppression, crying out, deliverance by a judge, peace, and then sin again
acceptable_variants: sin — oppression — cry — deliverance — rest — sin
the judges cycle of apostasy rescue and return to sin
four stages: Israel sins, God punishes, Israel repents, God delivers

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: A military deliverer raised by God to rescue Israel from foreign oppression; unlike later kings, judges did not inherit their role and Israel reverted to sin after each judge died
The Canaanite storm deity Israel repeatedly worshipped in violation of the covenant; the name appears repeatedly in Judges as the god that drew Israel away from Yahweh
